auth.loginDescription: Log in to continue your AI journey
auth.registerDescription: Start building your AI models today

dashboard.title: My Projects
dashboard.noProjectsDescription: Create your first project to start the LLM training experience

wizard.title: Project Wizard
wizard.description: Define your model's purpose, domain, language, and type to get started.
wizard.modelPurposeDescription: What will your model primarily be used for?
wizard.targetDomainDescription: Which domain will your model specialize in?
wizard.modelLanguageDescription: Which language(s) should your model support?
wizard.modelTypeDescription: What type of model do you want to build?

purposeHints.chatbot: Conversational AI that can engage in natural dialogue. Best for customer support, virtual assistants, and interactive applications.
purposeHints.code_assistant: Specialized in code generation, completion, and debugging. Trained on programming languages and documentation.
purposeHints.translation: Optimized for translating text between languages with high accuracy. Requires parallel corpus data.
purposeHints.summarization: Condenses long documents into brief, accurate summaries. Good for news, research papers, and reports.
purposeHints.general: A versatile model for multiple tasks. Jack of all trades, but may underperform specialized models.

domainHints.general: No specific domain focus. Suitable for general-purpose applications with diverse topics.
domainHints.medical: Trained on medical literature, clinical notes, and health data. Must handle terminology and safety carefully.
domainHints.legal: Focused on legal documents, contracts, and regulations. Requires precise language understanding.
domainHints.finance: Specialized in financial reports, market analysis, and economic data. Needs numerical reasoning.
domainHints.tech: Covers software engineering, IT documentation, and technical specifications.
domainHints.education: Optimized for educational content, tutoring, and learning materials across subjects.

languageHints.en: English-only model. Highest performance for English tasks with smaller training data requirements.
languageHints.tr: Turkish-only model. Requires Turkish-specific tokenizer for optimal performance.
languageHints.multilingual: Supports multiple languages. Larger vocabulary needed but enables cross-lingual transfer.

typeHints.chat: Fine-tuned for multi-turn conversations with system prompts and role-playing capabilities.
typeHints.code: Optimized for code generation with fill-in-the-middle training and repository-level context.
typeHints.instruct: Follows explicit instructions precisely. Best for task completion and structured outputs.
typeHints.base: Raw language model without alignment. Foundation for further fine-tuning and customization.

dataset.title: Dataset Lab
dataset.description: Upload and analyze your training dataset to assess its quality.
dataset.fileTypeDescription: What format is your dataset in?
dataset.fileSizeDescription: How large is your dataset file?
dataset.rowCountDescription: Number of samples in your dataset

cleaning.title: Data Cleaning
cleaning.description: Clean and preprocess your dataset to improve training quality.
cleaning.operationsDescription: Select which cleaning steps to apply to your dataset.
cleaning.removeDuplicatesDescription: Detect and remove duplicate entries from the dataset
cleaning.maskPiiDescription: Mask personally identifiable information for privacy compliance

tokenizer.title: Tokenizer Lab
tokenizer.description: Choose a tokenizer that best fits your model's language and use case.
tokenizer.selectTokenizerDescription: Each tokenizer has different efficiency and cost characteristics.

typeDescriptions.general: Standard tokenizer for English and general text
typeDescriptions.turkish_optimized: Optimized for Turkish morphology, 15% more efficient for TR
typeDescriptions.code: Handles code syntax, indentation, and programming patterns

architecture.title: Model Architecture
architecture.description: Design your model's architecture by selecting size, context window, and type.
architecture.modelSizeDescription: Larger models are more capable but require more resources.
architecture.contextWindowDescription: How many tokens the model can process at once.
architecture.architectureTypeDescription: Dense processes all parameters; MoE activates subsets for efficiency.

sizeDescriptions.small: 1 GPU, fast training, good for focused tasks

typeDescriptions.dense: All parameters active on every token. Simple and predictable.
typeDescriptions.moe: Activates subset of params per token. Faster training, more memory.

trainingConfig.title: Training Configuration
trainingConfig.description: Configure hyperparameters that control how your model learns.
trainingConfig.modeDescription: Beginner mode uses recommended defaults. Advanced gives full control.
trainingConfig.epochsDescription: Number of complete passes through the training data
trainingConfig.batchSizeDescription: Number of samples processed before updating model weights
trainingConfig.learningRateDescription: Step size for weight updates. Too high = unstable, too low = slow.
trainingConfig.optimizerDescription: Algorithm used to update model weights during training
trainingConfig.fp16Description: Reduces memory usage by ~40% with minimal quality loss
trainingConfig.presetApplied: Recommended defaults applied for your model configuration.

training.title: Training Simulator
training.description: Run the training simulation and observe how your model learns.

report.title: Base Model Report
report.description: Review your trained base model's performance across standard benchmarks.

customization.title: Customization Studio
customization.description: Choose a method to customize and improve your base model.
customization.selectMethodDescription: Each method has different strengths and trade-offs.

methodDescriptions.rag: Augment responses with retrieved external knowledge. No retraining needed.
methodDescriptions.finetune: Full fine-tuning on domain-specific data. Best accuracy improvement.
methodDescriptions.lora: Efficient adaptation with trainable low-rank matrices. Less compute needed.
methodDescriptions.instruction_tuning: Teach the model to follow instructions better. Great for chat models.

rag.title: RAG Simulator
rag.description: Configure and simulate a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline.
rag.chunkSizeDescription: Size of text chunks for indexing (smaller = more precise, larger = more context)
rag.topKDescription: Number of relevant chunks to retrieve per query

fineTune.title: Fine-Tune Simulator
fineTune.description: Simulate fine-tuning your model on domain-specific data.

playground.title: Playground
playground.description: Test your model variants with custom prompts and compare responses.
playground.promptPlaceholder: Type a message to test the model...
playground.noResponse: Send a prompt to see the model response.

evaluation.title: Evaluation Center
evaluation.description: Comprehensive evaluation of your model across key performance metrics.
evaluation.verdictGood: Your model performs well across all metrics. Ready for deployment.
evaluation.verdictMedium: Decent performance with room for improvement. Consider further fine-tuning.
evaluation.verdictPoor: Model needs significant improvements before deployment. Review training configuration.

deployment.title: Deployment Simulator
deployment.description: Simulate deploying your model as an API endpoint.
deployment.projectComplete: Congratulations! You have completed the full LLM training lifecycle.

landing.hero: The Best Place to Learn How LLMs Are Built
landing.heroDescription: Experience the full lifecycle of training and customizing a Large Language Model — from dataset preparation to deployment — without expensive infrastructure.
landing.feature1Description: Upload, clean, and tokenize your training data. See how data quality directly impacts model performance.
landing.feature2Description: Choose model size, context window, and architecture type. Understand the trade-offs between capability and cost.
landing.feature3Description: Watch loss curves, GPU usage, and training logs in real-time. Learn how hyperparameters affect convergence.
landing.feature4Description: Evaluate against MMLU, HellaSwag, TruthfulQA and more. Identify weaknesses and improve your model.
landing.feature5Description: Customize your model with retrieval-augmented generation, fine-tuning, LoRA, or instruction tuning.
landing.feature6Description: Test your model with custom prompts, compare variants, and simulate API deployment with cost estimates.
landing.step1: Configure your model's purpose, domain, and language
landing.step2: Prepare and clean your training dataset
landing.cta: Ready to Build Your AI Model?
landing.ctaDescription: Start your LLM training journey today. No GPU required.
landing.footer: Built for education. Powered by simulation.

wizard.body: Before writing a single line of code, real ML teams decide what the model is for. Purpose, domain, and language choices shape every downstream decision — dataset, tokenizer, size, cost. Think of it like planning a trip: you pick the destination before packing.

dataset.body: Models learn patterns from data — garbage in, garbage out. Format (JSONL/CSV), size, duplicates, and language balance all affect what the model will learn. A small clean dataset often beats a huge messy one.

cleaning.body: Raw data has duplicates, spam, and private information. Cleaning is like editing a rough manuscript: remove noise so the signal comes through. Quality improvements here compound through every later step.

tokenizer.body: Tokenization splits text into the units the model actually reads. Like chopping a sentence into LEGO bricks — smaller bricks = more flexibility but more pieces to track. A Turkish-tuned tokenizer can cut token count 15%+ for Turkish text.

architecture.body: Model size sets the capability ceiling — and the bill. A 1B model trains on one GPU in a day; a 70B needs 16 GPUs for a month. Context window is how much text the model can 'see' at once, like working memory.

trainingConfig.body: Hyperparameters control how the model learns. Learning rate is the step size — too big and the model overshoots; too small and it crawls. Batch size and epochs trade off stability and speed. Defaults work; tuning squeezes out the last few percent.

training.body: Training is an iterative search: show the model examples, measure error, nudge weights, repeat — millions of times. The loss curve is your heartbeat monitor. A healthy curve drops fast then plateaus; a flat or spiky one means something's wrong.

report.body: Benchmarks are standardized exams — MMLU for knowledge, GSM8K for math, TruthfulQA for honesty. Your base model's scores show its strengths and weaknesses before you invest in customization. Weak areas tell you what to target next.

customization.body: Base models are generalists. To specialize, you can fine-tune (reshape the model itself), use LoRA (cheap adapter layers), or RAG (let it look things up). Each trades cost, flexibility, and peak quality differently.

rag.body: RAG lets the model consult an external knowledge base at query time — like giving it an open-book exam. No retraining needed. Chunk size and top-K control how much context each question pulls in: too little misses answers, too much confuses.

fineTune.body: Fine-tuning continues training on your domain data. The model keeps what it knows but sharpens on your specifics. Small learning rates prevent catastrophic forgetting — you're adjusting, not rewriting.

playground.body: Real evaluation happens beyond benchmarks — does the model actually feel good to use? Compare base, fine-tuned, and RAG variants on the same prompts. You'll see quality, latency, and cost trade-offs in practice.

evaluation.body: A final multi-angle check before shipping: accuracy (is it right?), hallucination risk (does it invent things?), quality (is it useful?), latency (is it fast?). Real production models must balance all four — a genius that takes 10 seconds per reply is not deployable.

deployment.body: Deployment costs compound fast. GPU-hours for training are one-time; inference costs recur every single request. Estimating both up front tells you whether the business case actually works — before you pay the bill.
